ayahEnglish: About what are they asking one another?
That over which they are in disagreement.
That over which they are in disagreement.
That over which they are in disagreement.
Have We not made the earth a resting place?
Have We not made the earth a resting place?

targetTranslation: a smooth spread-out place (2nd object of نَجْعَلِ - what the earth was made into)
ayahEnglish: Have We not made the earth a resting place?
And the mountains as stakes?

targetTranslation: as pairs/spouses (object of خَلَقْنٰ - We created)
ayahEnglish: And We created you in pairs.
And made your sleep [a means for] rest

targetTranslation: as a deep rest (object of جَعَلْنَا)
ayahEnglish: And made your sleep [a means for] rest
And made the night as clothing.
And made the day for livelihood.

targetTranslation: above you all (object of وَبَنَيْنَا - and We built)
ayahEnglish: And constructed above you seven strong [heavens].
And constructed above you seven strong [heavens].
And constructed above you seven strong [heavens].
And made [therein] a burning lamp
And made [therein] a burning lamp

targetTranslation: water (object of أَنزَلْنَا - We sent down)
ayahEnglish: And sent down, from the rain clouds, pouring water.

targetTranslation: pouring forth very abundantly (describes مَآءً water)
ayahEnglish: And sent down, from the rain clouds, pouring water.

targetTranslation: grain/seed (object of نُخْرِجَ - we bring forth)
ayahEnglish: That We may bring forth thereby grain and vegetation.
And gardens of entwined growth.
Indeed, the Day of Judgement is an appointed time -
Indeed, the Day of Judgement is an appointed time -
Indeed, the Day of Judgement is an appointed time -

ayahArabic: يَوْمَ يُنفَخُ فِى ٱلصُّورِ فَتَأْتُونَ أَفْوَاجًا
ayahEnglish: The Day the Horn is blown and you will come forth in multitudes

ayahArabic: يَوْمَ يُنفَخُ فِى ٱلصُّورِ فَتَأْتُونَ أَفْوَاجًا
ayahEnglish: The Day the Horn is blown and you will come forth in multitudes

targetTranslation: in groups (describing how people will come on the Day of Judgment)
ayahArabic: يَوْمَ يُنفَخُ فِى ٱلصُّورِ فَتَأْتُونَ أَفْوَاجًا
ayahEnglish: The Day the Horn is blown and you will come forth in multitudes
And the heaven is opened and will become gateways.

targetTranslation: a mirage (describing what the mountains became)
ayahEnglish: And the mountains are removed and will be [but] a mirage.
Indeed, Hell has been lying in wait
Indeed, Hell has been lying in wait

targetTranslation: a place of watching/ambush (as the predicate of كَانَتْ)
ayahEnglish: Indeed, Hell has been lying in wait

targetTranslation: a place of return (for الطَّاغِينَ - the transgressors)
ayahEnglish: For the transgressors, a place of return,

targetTranslation: surely staying/remaining (describes the people in Hell)
ayahEnglish: In which they will remain for ages [unending].

targetTranslation: long periods of time (describing how long they will stay)
ayahEnglish: In which they will remain for ages [unending].

targetTranslation: they taste (referring to the disbelievers in Hell)
ayahArabic: لَّا يَذُوقُونَ فِيهَا بَرْدًا وَلَا شَرَابًا
ayahEnglish: They will not taste therein [any] coolness or drink.

targetTranslation: any coolness (object of يَذُوقُونَ - they taste)
ayahArabic: لَّا يَذُوقُونَ فِيهَا بَرْدًا وَلَا شَرَابًا
ayahEnglish: They will not taste therein [any] coolness or drink.

ayahArabic: لَّا يَذُوقُونَ فِيهَا بَرْدًا وَلَا شَرَابًا
ayahEnglish: They will not taste therein [any] coolness or drink.
Except scalding water and [foul] purulence -

targetTranslation: a repayment (as the object being given)
ayahEnglish: An appropriate recompense.

targetTranslation: as a fitting consequence (describes جَزَآءً - recompense)
ayahEnglish: An appropriate recompense.

targetTranslation: they were (subject of لَا يَرْجُونَ - they do not expect)
ayahEnglish: Indeed, they were not expecting an account
Indeed, they were not expecting an account

targetTranslation: a reckoning (object of لَا يَرْجُونَ - they were not expecting)
ayahEnglish: Indeed, they were not expecting an account

targetTranslation: an intense/habitual liar (object of كَذَّبُوا/they denied)
ayahEnglish: And denied Our verses with [emphatic] denial.
But all things We have enumerated in writing.

targetTranslation: we counted it completely (referring to كُلَّ شَىْءٍ - everything)
ayahEnglish: But all things We have enumerated in writing.
But all things We have enumerated in writing.

targetTranslation: we will surely increase you all
ayahEnglish: "So taste [the penalty], and never will We increase you except in torment."

targetTranslation: a punishment (object of نَّزِيدَكُمْ - we will increase you)
ayahEnglish: "So taste [the penalty], and never will We increase you except in torment."

targetTranslation: a place of success (object of إِنَّ)
ayahEnglish: Indeed, for the righteous is attainment -
Gardens and grapevines.
And full-breasted [companions] of equal age.
And a full cup.

targetTranslation: they hear/are hearing (referring to the disbelievers in Hell)
ayahArabic: لَّا يَسْمَعُونَ فِيهَا لَغْوًا وَلَا كِذَّابًا
ayahEnglish: No ill speech will they hear therein or any falsehood -

targetTranslation: empty/useless talk (object of يَسْمَعُونَ - they hear)
ayahArabic: لَّا يَسْمَعُونَ فِيهَا لَغْوًا وَلَا كِذَّابًا
ayahEnglish: No ill speech will they hear therein or any falsehood -

targetTranslation: a liar (being described in لَّا يَسْمَعُونَ)
ayahArabic: لَّا يَسْمَعُونَ فِيهَا لَغْوًا وَلَا كِذَّابًا
ayahEnglish: No ill speech will they hear therein or any falsehood -

targetTranslation: a reward/compensation (as the object of عَطَآءً - a gift)
ayahEnglish: [As] reward from your Lord, [a generous] gift [made due by] account,
[As] reward from your Lord, [a generous] gift [made due by] account,

targetTranslation: a gift (as the object of جَزَآءً/reward)
ayahEnglish: [As] reward from your Lord, [a generous] gift [made due by] account,
[As] reward from your Lord, [a generous] gift [made due by] account,

targetTranslation: Lord of (connected to السَّمٰوٰتِ - the heavens)
ayahArabic: رَّبِّ ٱلسَّمٰوٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَا ٱلرَّحْمٰنِ لَا يَمْلِكُونَ مِنْهُ خِطَابًا
ayahEnglish: [From] the Lord of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them, the Most Merciful. They possess not from Him [authority for] speech.

ayahArabic: رَّبِّ ٱلسَّمٰوٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَا ٱلرَّحْمٰنِ لَا يَمْلِكُونَ مِنْهُ خِطَابًا
ayahEnglish: [From] the Lord of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them, the Most Merciful. They possess not from Him [authority for] speech.

targetTranslation: they possess/have power over (referring to those being addressed in لَا يَمْلِكُونَ - they do not have power)
ayahArabic: رَّبِّ ٱلسَّمٰوٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَا ٱلرَّحْمٰنِ لَا يَمْلِكُونَ مِنْهُ خِطَابًا
ayahEnglish: [From] the Lord of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them, the Most Merciful. They possess not from Him [authority for] speech.

targetTranslation: any direct speech/address (being acted upon by يَمْلِكُونَ - they possess)
ayahArabic: رَّبِّ ٱلسَّمٰوٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَا ٱلرَّحْمٰنِ لَا يَمْلِكُونَ مِنْهُ خِطَابًا
ayahEnglish: [From] the Lord of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them, the Most Merciful. They possess not from Him [authority for] speech.

targetTranslation: on the Day (when يَقُومُ the Spirit stands)
ayahArabic: يَوْمَ يَقُومُ ٱلرُّوحُ وَٱلْمَلٰٓئِكَةُ صَفًّا لَّا يَتَكَلَّمُونَ إِلَّا مَنْ أَذِنَ لَهُ ٱلرَّحْمٰنُ وَقَالَ صَوَابًا
ayahEnglish: The Day that the Spirit [i.e., Gabriel] and the angels will stand in rows, they will not speak except for one whom the Most Merciful permits, and he will say what is correct.

ayahArabic: يَوْمَ يَقُومُ ٱلرُّوحُ وَٱلْمَلٰٓئِكَةُ صَفًّا لَّا يَتَكَلَّمُونَ إِلَّا مَنْ أَذِنَ لَهُ ٱلرَّحْمٰنُ وَقَالَ صَوَابًا
ayahEnglish: The Day that the Spirit [i.e., Gabriel] and the angels will stand in rows, they will not speak except for one whom the Most Merciful permits, and he will say what is correct.

targetTranslation: in a row/line (describing how the Spirit and angels will stand)
ayahArabic: يَوْمَ يَقُومُ ٱلرُّوحُ وَٱلْمَلٰٓئِكَةُ صَفًّا لَّا يَتَكَلَّمُونَ إِلَّا مَنْ أَذِنَ لَهُ ٱلرَّحْمٰنُ وَقَالَ صَوَابًا
ayahEnglish: The Day that the Spirit [i.e., Gabriel] and the angels will stand in rows, they will not speak except for one whom the Most Merciful permits, and he will say what is correct.

ayahArabic: يَوْمَ يَقُومُ ٱلرُّوحُ وَٱلْمَلٰٓئِكَةُ صَفًّا لَّا يَتَكَلَّمُونَ إِلَّا مَنْ أَذِنَ لَهُ ٱلرَّحْمٰنُ وَقَالَ صَوَابًا
ayahEnglish: The Day that the Spirit [i.e., Gabriel] and the angels will stand in rows, they will not speak except for one whom the Most Merciful permits, and he will say what is correct.

ayahArabic: يَوْمَ يَقُومُ ٱلرُّوحُ وَٱلْمَلٰٓئِكَةُ صَفًّا لَّا يَتَكَلَّمُونَ إِلَّا مَنْ أَذِنَ لَهُ ٱلرَّحْمٰنُ وَقَالَ صَوَابًا
ayahEnglish: The Day that the Spirit [i.e., Gabriel] and the angels will stand in rows, they will not speak except for one whom the Most Merciful permits, and he will say what is correct.

ayahArabic: يَوْمَ يَقُومُ ٱلرُّوحُ وَٱلْمَلٰٓئِكَةُ صَفًّا لَّا يَتَكَلَّمُونَ إِلَّا مَنْ أَذِنَ لَهُ ٱلرَّحْمٰنُ وَقَالَ صَوَابًا
ayahEnglish: The Day that the Spirit [i.e., Gabriel] and the angels will stand in rows, they will not speak except for one whom the Most Merciful permits, and he will say what is correct.

targetTranslation: what is correct/right (object of قَالَ - he said)
ayahArabic: يَوْمَ يَقُومُ ٱلرُّوحُ وَٱلْمَلٰٓئِكَةُ صَفًّا لَّا يَتَكَلَّمُونَ إِلَّا مَنْ أَذِنَ لَهُ ٱلرَّحْمٰنُ وَقَالَ صَوَابًا
ayahEnglish: The Day that the Spirit [i.e., Gabriel] and the angels will stand in rows, they will not speak except for one whom the Most Merciful permits, and he will say what is correct.

ayahArabic: ذٰلِكَ ٱلْيَوْمُ ٱلْحَقُّ فَمَن شَآءَ ٱتَّخَذَ إِلَىٰ رَبِّهِۦ مَـَٔابًا
ayahEnglish: That is the True [i.e., certain] Day; so he who wills may take to his Lord a [way of] return.

ayahArabic: ذٰلِكَ ٱلْيَوْمُ ٱلْحَقُّ فَمَن شَآءَ ٱتَّخَذَ إِلَىٰ رَبِّهِۦ مَـَٔابًا
ayahEnglish: That is the True [i.e., certain] Day; so he who wills may take to his Lord a [way of] return.

ayahArabic: ذٰلِكَ ٱلْيَوْمُ ٱلْحَقُّ فَمَن شَآءَ ٱتَّخَذَ إِلَىٰ رَبِّهِۦ مَـَٔابًا
ayahEnglish: That is the True [i.e., certain] Day; so he who wills may take to his Lord a [way of] return.

ayahArabic: ذٰلِكَ ٱلْيَوْمُ ٱلْحَقُّ فَمَن شَآءَ ٱتَّخَذَ إِلَىٰ رَبِّهِۦ مَـَٔابًا
ayahEnglish: That is the True [i.e., certain] Day; so he who wills may take to his Lord a [way of] return.

targetTranslation: a place of return (object being described)
ayahArabic: ذٰلِكَ ٱلْيَوْمُ ٱلْحَقُّ فَمَن شَآءَ ٱتَّخَذَ إِلَىٰ رَبِّهِۦ مَـَٔابًا
ayahEnglish: That is the True [i.e., certain] Day; so he who wills may take to his Lord a [way of] return.

targetTranslation: we warned you all (referring to the disbelievers being warned of punishment)
ayahArabic: إِنَّآ أَنذَرْنٰكُمْ عَذَابًا قَرِيبًا يَوْمَ يَنظُرُ ٱلْمَرْءُ مَا قَدَّمَتْ يَدَاهُ وَيَقُولُ ٱلْكَافِرُ يٰلَيْتَنِى كُنتُ تُرٰبًۢا
ayahEnglish: Indeed, We have warned you of an impending punishment on the Day when a man will observe what his hands have put forth and the disbeliever will say, "Oh, I wish that I were dust!"

ayahArabic: إِنَّآ أَنذَرْنٰكُمْ عَذَابًا قَرِيبًا يَوْمَ يَنظُرُ ٱلْمَرْءُ مَا قَدَّمَتْ يَدَاهُ وَيَقُولُ ٱلْكَافِرُ يٰلَيْتَنِى كُنتُ تُرٰبًۢا
